meaning: adj.没法辩解的,不可宽赦的
examples: He said the killing of innocent people was inexcusable.
Conditions are good in school. It's inexcusable not to study hard.
That would be inexcusable, and entirely self-inflicted by Washington.
Look at the words he's used: "outrageous", "intolerable", "inexcusable", and if you didn't get it, "wrong".
This is clearly inexcusable and something for which my law educators must be held to account.
